articles.title: THE 2025 DORA DATA SHOWS
articles.summary: Most 10x AI productivity claims don't hold up in real teams. The 2025 DORA report [4] — with Nathen Harvey (Google Cloud) and Justin Reock (DX) — reveals what's moving the needle.

articles.title: Watch on demand →
MAINTAINABILITY SENSORS FOR CODING AGENTS
articles.summary: AI coding agents use maintainability sensors like linting and type checking to self-correct quality issues, as these tools provide an automated feedback loop for handling things like complex logic and excessive function length. Despite reducing manual overhead, human oversight is still necessary for preventing over-engineered solutions and addressing deeper semantic quality concerns.

articles.title: INVERTING PHOTODNA
articles.summary: Microsoft's PhotoDNA uses digital signatures to identify illegal imagery based on the claim that these hashes are irreversible, but a machine learning tool named Ribosome successfully inverted them to reconstruct recognizable images. This shows that PhotoDNA signatures leak a lot of image information.

articles.title: DUMB WAYS FOR AN OPEN SOURCE PROJECT TO DIE
articles.summary: It's common for open source projects to turn into "zombies,” still used but totally abandoned, because of issues like maintainer burnout, funding problems, or old, broken tech. Since these packages are still listed everywhere, they pose a secret but major risk to all the software that depends on them.

articles.title: THE CHEAP, GOOD, AND FAST PARADOX RETURNS
articles.summary: AI is challenging the long-standing project management paradox that work cannot be cheap, good, and fast simultaneously by accelerating the technical coding phase. Even with these advancements, human resistance to new tools and complex regulatory hurdles like SOC 2 compliance continue to prevent an easier development process.

articles.title: YOUR USERS EXPERIENCE EVERYTHING. YOUR OBSERVABILITY SHOULD TOO.
articles.summary: Your customer lives in the real world, and it's messy: Intermittent connections, mid-onboarding drop-offs, force quits... Unlike other observability tools, bitdrift captures 100% of real-time data, unsampled across 1B+ installs. With our Public API and agent skills, your agents query that reality directly.

articles.title: EXPLORE THE BITDRIFT SANDBOX.
EMPOWER YOUR AI AGENTS to query reality with bitdrift skills.
GEMINI 3.5: FRONTIER INTELLIGENCE WITH ACTION
articles.summary: Gemini 3.5 is a large upgrade of the Gemini model family, kicking off with the super-fast 3.5 Flash model that handles complex tasks right in apps like Search. It performs well on a majority of benchmarks and outputs tokens faster than competing models.

articles.title: THE GEMINI APP BECOMES MORE AGENTIC, DELIVERING PROACTIVE, 24/7 HELP
articles.summary: The new Gemini 3.5 Flash model provides a faster and more engaging AI experience overall. Users of the Gemini app now have new features like cinematic video generation, personalized daily summaries, and a 24/7 agent (Gemini Spark) to handle complex tasks.

articles.title: A NEW ERA FOR AI SEARCH
articles.summary: Google Search is getting its biggest update ever, integrating Gemini 3.5 Flash into a new AI search box to introduce background agents that can automate tasks like booking. Plus, Google is adding agentic coding tools and personal intelligence features that securely connect your private data from apps like Gmail and Calendar for tailored results.

articles.title: ANTHROPIC IS PREPARING FOR IPO AND WE SHOULD BE WORRIED
articles.summary: Anthropic seems to be getting ready for an IPO by making its access more restrictive, like limiting 'claude -p' and Agent SDK usage, and cutting off third-party tools and competitor APIs. These actions, plus their major $200M DoD deal, show Anthropic is moving away from its public-interest branding toward being a closed, monetization-focused company.

articles.title: ADVANCING CONTENT PROVENANCE FOR A SAFER, MORE TRANSPARENT AI ECOSYSTEM
articles.summary: To make AI media transparent, we're using a multi-layered strategy that includes C2PA metadata and cryptographic signatures so you can easily verify the origin. This system also embeds invisible SynthID watermarks into images for resilience, and a public verification tool is available to check for all these signals.

articles.title: MECHANIZE IS HIRING SOFTWARE ENGINEERS AND INTERNS
articles.summary: We build environments and evals for frontier coding agents. We pay junior engineers $300k base and high performers earn large bonuses. Applying takes two minutes:

articles.title: Apply Now
ANDREJ KARPATHY JOINS ANTHROPIC
articles.summary: Andrej Karpathy has joined Anthropic to focus on frontier LLM research and development while planning to eventually resume work on educational initiatives.

articles.title: APPLE UNVEILS NEW ACCESSIBILITY FEATURES, AND UPDATES WITH APPLE INTELLIGENCE
articles.summary: Apple has unveiled a series of accessibility updates powered by Apple Intelligence, including better navigation tools, automated video subtitles, and eye-controlled wheelchair support for Apple Vision Pro.

articles.title: REMOVE AI WATERMARKS
articles.summary: This Python-based program provides a CLI and library for removing visible logos, invisible digital watermarks, and AI-related provenance metadata from images created by various generative models.

articles.title: AN IMPORTANT UPDATE: TRANSITIONING GEMINI CLI TO ANTIGRAVITY CLI
articles.summary: Google is transitioning Gemini CLI to the unified, agent-first Antigravity CLI platform, with support for non-enterprise versions of Gemini CLI set to end on June 18.
